title: Yas Marina Circuit
sub: The season finale: a twilight-to-night race on Yas Island, reprofiled in 2021 into a faster, flowing lap. Immaculate, glamorous, and the stage where more than one championship has been settled on the last day.

sectors.name: Sector 1
sectors.char: The Turn 1 left off the start, a short straight to the Turn 2-3 sequence, then the long straight into the Turn 5 braking zone. Two DRS zones make this the racy part of the lap.

sectors.name: Sector 2
sectors.char: The 2021-reprofiled flowing section: the fast Turn 6-7 sweep (formerly a slow hairpin) and the run around the marina, now taken at much higher speed.

sectors.name: Sector 3
sectors.char: The technical run along the marina and under the W hotel, easing corners replacing the old chicanes, then the final sweeps onto the start/finish straight.

tyreAlloc: Softest in the range · C3 / C4 / C5
stratNote: Smooth asphalt and cool evening temperatures keep degradation low, so it is usually a one-stopper. The track rubbers in and the temperature drops as the sun sets, so late runners in qualifying and the second stint often find more grip.
spots: End of the long straight + DRS. The heaviest braking zone and the main passing spot.
Off the start straight + DRS; an opening-lap and secondary chance.
A late dive after the fast marina sweeps.
otNote: The 2021 reprofile (21 corners down to 16) sped up the flowing sections and helped following, and two early DRS zones give real chances into Turn 5 and Turn 1. Still not a passing paradise, but far better than the old layout.
corners: Slow left off the start straight + DRS. An opening-lap flashpoint and a secondary passing spot.
Quick corners linking to the long straight.
Flat-out kink on the run down to Turn 5.
End of the long straight + DRS. The lap’s biggest stop and the prime overtaking spot.
The reprofiled fast sweep (a slow hairpin pre-2021), now taken at high speed.
A late-brake chance after the sweeps.
Medium corners under the W hotel.
Eased, faster corners replacing the old hotel chicane.
Final left firing cars onto the start/finish straight and DRS 1.
geo: The season closer starts in daylight and finishes under floodlights, the sky fading over Yas Island. A glamorous, high-stakes end to the year.
The circuit was cut from 21 corners to 16 for 2021, replacing slow chicanes with faster, flowing corners to improve overtaking. Lap times dropped by more than 10 seconds.
Yas Marina has hosted several championship showdowns, most infamously the 2021 last-lap finale. Being the finale, it often carries the year’s biggest stakes.
An immaculate, purpose-built venue with a hotel straddling the track, a subterranean pit exit, and the only F1 circuit with fully covered grandstands.
